nodes.en: Disaster response encompasses organized procedures for mitigating impacts of large-scale catastrophic events—earthquakes, floods, hurricanes—through coordinated deployment of resources, evacuation, and recovery operations.
nodes.cs: Reakce na katastrofu zahrnuje organizované procedury ke zmírnění dopadů velkoplošných katastrofických událostí—zemětřesení, povodně, hurikány—prostřednictvím koordinovaného nasazení zdrojů, evakuace a obnovy.
nodes.brief:
Disaster response encompasses organized procedures for mitigating impacts of large-scale catastrophic events—earthquakes, floods, hurricanes—through coordinated deployment of resources, evacuation, and recovery operations. It operates at a different scale than most of this branch: where other subfields equip an individual or household, disaster response coordinates households, neighborhoods, and institutions together.

Within [[08]] Survival & Emergency Preparedness it draws directly on [[08.01]] First Aid & Medical for mass-casualty triage and on [[08.07]] Emergency Communication for coordinating evacuation and relief across a disrupted area; it also depends on [[08.10]] Psychological Resilience, since sustained recovery operations test responders and survivors over weeks rather than hours. Compared with [[08.08]] Defense & Security, which addresses threats from other people, this subfield addresses threats from the environment itself.

For evacuation planning and hazard-specific guidance, see the U.S. [Federal Emergency Management Agency](https://www.ready.gov/), and for international disaster-response coordination standards, the [International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies](https://www.ifrc.org/).
